key_insight: A layerwise self-adaptive PINN improves training balance across residual, initial, and boundary terms, enabling better capture of sharp shock fronts in high-Reynolds multidimensional coupled Burgers’ equations.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a more stable mesh-free alternative for simulating nonlinear transport with steep gradients, where classical finite-difference and finite-element solvers struggle.
method: Layerwise self-adaptive weighting updates penalty terms during training.
Dual-phase optimization is used to improve convergence stability.
Compared against standard PINN variants with and without L-BFGS.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments report lower relative L2 error than baseline PINNs and improved ability to track shock-front formation over time.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful tuning of adaptive weights and optimization stages, especially for stiff high-Re problems.
paper_title: MATLAB-Based Layerwise Self-Adaptive Physics-Informed Neural Network in Applications to Multidimensional Coupled Burgers' Equations with High Reynolds Numbers
